timeline.text: Airflow — nightly-revenue failed at stg_orders: OperationalError: no such column: o.order_total (0 of 3 marts refreshed, finance dashboard is stale)

timeline.cmd: SELECT cid, name, type FROM pragma_table_info('orders_raw');
timeline.out:
cid  name          type
---  ------------  -------
0    order_id      INTEGER
1    customer_id   INTEGER
2    order_ts      TEXT
3    total_amount  REAL
4    status        TEXT
5    load_batch    TEXT
timeline.note: what does the raw table actually look like now?

timeline.cause: Upstream schema drift: the vendor's export renamed `order_total` to `total_amount`, so the staging build now references a column that no longer exists and the whole pipeline halts at stg_orders. The raw data itself arrived fine — only the contract moved.
receipts: alternative untested: delayed upstream compatibility view

timeline.command:
--- tasks/stg_orders.sql
+++ tasks/stg_orders.sql
- o.order_total AS order_total
+ o.total_amount AS order_total   -- vendor renamed the column
timeline.explain: Alias the new column name back to `order_total` in staging, so every downstream table keeps its contract. One line, rebuilds staging from raw — no source data touched.
timeline.rollback: Reverse the patch: `o.total_amount AS order_total` → `o.order_total AS order_total`, re-run the pipeline.
timeline.followup: Add a schema contract check at load time so a renamed column pages BEFORE the nightly run, not during it.

timeline.out:
--- tasks/stg_orders.sql
+++ tasks/stg_orders.sql
- o.order_total AS order_total
+ o.total_amount AS order_total   -- vendor renamed the column

timeline.text: All tasks rebuilt, every check green — marts are fresh and the numbers reconcile. Standing down.

title: nightly-revenue derailed — vendor renamed a column overnight
